title: A Face
content:
A face in the mist, with rain around,
    clings to bare leaves frowning.
    A face through the mist, convulsed,
    plays stationary, perching from twigs.
    A face, not knowing it, trust it is good.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "A Face", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
